blocks.purpose: Lighter-weight benefits grid — 3-8 icon+title+description items with no card chrome. Similar to LearningOutcomes but uses accent-colored inline icons and flows into the page background instead of boxed cards. Prefer for "Why attend" or "What makes this different" sections on sales/upsell pages.

exampleProps.headline: Four reasons this summit is unlike any you’ve joined before

benefits.title: Practitioners, not performers
benefits.description: Every speaker works with families every week. No career keynote-circuit people.

benefits.title: Watch on your schedule
benefits.description: 24-hour replay windows per day so bedtime doesn’t beat you.

benefits.title: Fully virtual, globally free
benefits.description: Join from wherever, on whatever. No travel, no hotels, no registration fee.

benefits.title: Curated, not kitchen-sink
benefits.description: No filler panels. Every session earned its place.

blocks.purpose: Longer-form benefits section — 2-4 benefits alternating left/right, each with a supporting lifestyle/scenario image. Use when individual benefits deserve more breathing room than a tight grid (50-100 words of copy per benefit).

benefits.title: A morning routine that survives the school run
benefits.description: Step-by-step morning templates built around what actually works with ADHD kids — visual timers, choice-based transitions, and zero nagging. Printable and kid-tested.

benefits.title: Conversations that land instead of lectures that don’t
benefits.description: Specific language patterns for the top 10 flashpoints (homework, screens, bedtime, sibling conflicts) so you stop repeating yourself and they start hearing you.

benefits.title: A calm-down toolkit you can actually pull out mid-meltdown
benefits.description: Three concrete techniques (co-regulation, sensory breaks, name-it-to-tame-it) that work in the heat of the moment. Not just theory — scripts included.

blocks.purpose: Bonus offer stack — 1-8 exclusive bonuses with title, description, optional value chip ("($19 value)"), and optional image. Classic "Register today and also receive..." section. Ends with a single CTA. Use before checkout to stack perceived value.

exampleProps.headline: Four exclusive bonuses — only when you register by the start of the summit
exampleProps.introText: These are included with every ticket and disappear when the summit goes live.

bonuses.title: Parenting Playbook PDF
bonuses.description: 120-page actionable guide distilling the summit’s key strategies by age group.

bonuses.title: Live Q&A with Day 1 Speakers
bonuses.description: Join speakers live for an open Q&A on focus and attention strategies.

bonuses.title: Private Community Access
bonuses.description: 90 days in a moderated community of 1,000+ parents working through the same tools.

bonuses.title: Morning Routine Templates
bonuses.description: Printable morning routine boards designed specifically for ADHD kids.

blocks.purpose: Final call-to-action block — headline + optional body + 2-10 checkmark bullets + prominent CTA. Use at the end of long landing pages or right before the fold of a sales page to summarize what the visitor is about to get.

exampleProps.headline: Here’s everything you get when you register
exampleProps.bodyText: One click, 30 seconds. We’ll email you the schedule and the first-day session link.
bullets: 5 days of live summit sessions with 40+ experts
24-hour replay on every session — watch when your kid is asleep
Live Q&A windows with the day’s speakers
Day 1 welcome guide + printable session planner
Private community for 90 days after the summit

blocks.purpose: Expandable FAQ section with 1-30 Q&A pairs. Built on the shadcn Accordion primitive. Answers support paragraph breaks via blank lines. Keep answers under ~200 words; long answers signal the product isn’t explaining itself earlier in the page.

items.question: How much does it cost to attend?
items.answer: Attendance is completely free. We cover all speakers and platform costs; you can optionally purchase lifetime access to the recordings.

items.question: What if I can’t watch live?
items.answer: Every session has a 24-hour replay window. If you miss it entirely, lifetime access (a paid upgrade) lets you watch on your schedule.

items.question: Do I need special equipment?
items.answer: Just a phone, tablet, or laptop with a browser. All sessions stream directly — no apps to install.

items.question: Will the speakers answer my questions?
items.answer: Yes. Every session ends with a live Q&A window, and we curate questions from attendees in real time.

items.question: Is this appropriate for parents of neurotypical kids?
items.answer: Most tools apply to any kid — ADHD is the lens, but the strategies around focus, regulation, and communication translate broadly.

blocks.purpose: Two-column feature section — headline + body + supporting image. Image flips to left or right per imagePosition. Use for "What is this summit?" or "About the offer" explanations. Body supports line breaks via blank lines; plain text only (no markdown rendering yet).

exampleProps.headline: What is ADHD Parenting Summit 2026?
exampleProps.bodyRich:
A reframed approach — 5 days instead of 3, 40+ experts, deeper understanding of your ADHD child.

From diagnosis to daily routines, you’ll leave with tools that actually work at home.

blocks.purpose: Page footer with logo, tagline, up to 10 legal/policy links, copyright, and an optional summit-dates reminder line. Goes on every funnel page. Links accept full URLs or anchors/paths — no schema enforcement so the footer can link to in-page sections as well as privacy/terms pages.

exampleProps.tagline: Parent-built tools for raising kids with ADHD, ages 4-16.

blocks.purpose: About-the-organizers section — 1-4 founder headshots on one side, mission-statement body copy + optional CTA on the other. Use to put a human face on the event before asking for registration. Keep body under ~120 words; readers skim this.

exampleProps.headline: Built by parents, for parents
exampleProps.bodyRich:
We ran our first summit in 2022 because we were drowning — two kids with ADHD, zero sleep, and no clear playbook. What we found was that the right experts, in the right order, could change everything.

This year’s summit is the fifth iteration. Every session earned its spot.

blocks.purpose: Above-the-fold hero for summit optins. Large headline + subhead + live countdown timer + primary/secondary CTAs + speaker count callout. Use for time-sensitive summit launches.

exampleProps.headline: WORLD'S LARGEST ADHD PARENTING SUMMIT
bodyLines: Covering focus, impulsivity, outbursts & screen management

blocks.purpose: Icon grid of 3-9 learning outcomes or key benefits. Each item: icon (from a fixed lucide set — heart/target/book/bolt/message/users), short title, 1-2 sentence description. Use after the hero to answer "what will I get out of this?" in scannable form.

exampleProps.headline: Five days of practical, science-backed parenting tools

items.title: Focus & Attention
items.description: Strategies that actually work for the butterfly mind.

items.title: Emotional Regulation
items.description: How to handle meltdowns, shutdowns, and big feelings.

items.title: Executive Function
items.description: Planning, prioritizing, and getting started — without the fight.

items.title: Communication
items.description: Language that lands instead of lectures that don’t.

items.title: Screen Management
items.description: Reset the family relationship with devices, without World War III.

blocks.purpose: Horizontal logo strip for press/publication or partner mentions. Choose "scroll" for a continuous marquee when you have many logos (6+); choose "static" for a tight centered row with 3–6 logos. Logos render desaturated and lift to full color on hover.

3-7 reasons in a numbered list with large display numerals beside each title + optional description. Classic "5 Reasons Why" format. Use when the reasons are worth reading in order rather than scanning as a grid.

exampleProps.eyebrow: 5 reasons parents return year after year
exampleProps.headline: Why this summit keeps landing

reasons.title: Every session is practical, not theoretical
reasons.description: You leave each day with something you can try at dinner tonight.

reasons.title: Speakers answer live questions
reasons.description: Every expert does a live Q&A window so you can ask about your specific kid.

reasons.title: Built around real parent schedules
reasons.description: 24-hour replay on every session so bedtime doesn’t beat you.

reasons.title: Speakers who see kids, not just citations
reasons.description: Every presenter runs an active practice. Research + real families.

reasons.title: Free to attend, free to keep the core sessions

blocks.purpose: Email + optional name optin form. Submits to Laravel POST /api/optin with the current funnel/step context. Required on any optin step — without this block, the page has no conversion mechanism. Three background styles for different page placements (hero-adjacent "primary", mid-page "light", bottom-of-page "dark").

exampleProps.headline: Reserve your free seat
exampleProps.subheadline: We’ll email you the schedule and day 1 session link.
exampleProps.secondaryText: Grab your seat before the summit starts
exampleProps.privacyText: We’ll send summit updates and occasional parenting resources. Unsubscribe anytime with one click.

blocks.purpose: Single-line social proof badge with an optional icon, a large highlight number/word, and a short descriptor. Place directly under the hero to establish trust via volume (e.g. "73,124 committed parents"). Keep the descriptor short and specific — generic praise ("trusted by thousands") reads as filler.

Speaker grid for a single day of the summit. Shows the day label, the day’s theme, and 3-12 speaker cards (headshot, name, credentials, session title). If speakerIds is omitted, the block falls back to every speaker whose dayNumber matches — so AI/editors can just set the day and let content flow. Use once per day.

Three-column stats bar for summit highlights — "X days / Y speakers / Z attendees". Large value on top, short descriptor underneath, optional icon above each. Exactly 3 stats; use other blocks for longer numeric lists.

Fixed, full-width bar pinned to the top or bottom of the viewport with a short urgency message, live countdown, and a single CTA. Use on time-sensitive summit pages to keep the deadline visible while visitors scroll. Auto-hides after the target when hideWhenExpired is true.

Rotating testimonial carousel with 3-30 customer/parent testimonials. Each slide: long-form quote, author name, optional role, optional photo, optional 1-5 star rating. Auto-advances by default (pause on hover). Use instead of TestimonialGrid when quotes are long and you want one to breathe at a time.

exampleProps.headline: Parents who’ve walked the path you’re on

testimonials.quote: I stopped yelling at homework time within the first week. The morning-routine session alone was worth the entire summit — my 9-year-old now actually gets herself out the door.
The emotional regulation day rewired how I respond to meltdowns. I was the parent adding fuel to the fire. I’m not anymore.
We’d been to three “parenting ADHD” trainings before this one. This is the first one where every session was actually actionable that night.

blocks.purpose: Embedded video testimonials — 1-6 YouTube/Vimeo embeds with title + optional speaker name. Use "single" for one prominent video; "grid" for a 2-column gallery of shorter clips. embedUrl must be the iframe embed URL (https://www.youtube.com/embed/ID or https://player.vimeo.com/video/ID), not the share URL.

exampleProps.headline: What past attendees are saying
exampleProps.subheadline: Real parents, real results after applying what they learned.

videos.title: I finally stopped yelling at homework time
The morning routine that saved us

blocks.purpose: Problem-framing statistics grid for "why this matters" sections — 3-8 scary/motivating stats with icon, big number/phrase, and a short explanatory label. Use before a solution pitch; pairs well after the hero.

exampleProps.headline: ADHD in kids is not a phase — it’s a quiet epidemic
exampleProps.introText: The numbers most parents don’t see until they’re already in the middle of it.

stats.label: U.S. children have been diagnosed with ADHD by age 17.
Higher risk of anxiety and depression in untreated kids with ADHD.
Of kids with ADHD will be held back at least one grade.
Of parents report significant marital stress over ADHD parenting.
